name: AJ Dybantsa
teamFit: Fits switch-heavy groups that need a wing connector next to on-ball talent.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Wins space with size, burst, and shot-creation fluidity.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Can carry high-usage reps without defaulting into chaos.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Capable secondary creation tool, still less advanced than the scoring package.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Pull-up threat already bends coverages.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Size and spacing utility keep him playable beside other creators.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Reads are generally quick enough for star-usage projection.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Star-size wing scoring scales cleanly into almost any lineup.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Physical tools give him real multi-matchup value.

summary.synopsis: Premier jumbo wing prospect with real primary-creator upside and clean two-way scalability.
strengths: Star wing size with self-creation juice.
Jumper pressure already forces defensive concessions.
Can play on or off the ball without shrinking the lineup.
weaknesses: Playmaking still projects better as strong secondary than elite table-setting.
Shot diet can drift difficult when he hunts tough pull-ups.
swingFactors: Whether the passing creation reaches clear lead-engine level.
How consistently the defensive tools become possession-to-possession impact.

projection.bestOutcome: All-NBA caliber scoring wing who can anchor offense without losing defensive size.
projection.medianOutcome: High-end first-option or elite co-star wing creator.
projection.riskSummary: The ceiling is tied to whether the processing and passing fully catch up to the scoring burden.

name: Darryn Peterson
teamFit: Best with shooting around him and a secondary wing defender handling the toughest perimeter assignment.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: One of the better downhill and separation creators in the class.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Shotmaking confidence can outrun possession control at times.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Can generate real passing value once the paint is bent.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Jumper threat is a real driver of his offensive profile.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Still strongest with the ball rather than as a pure connector.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Enough to handle creator reps, but not yet fully surgical.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Works best if paired with size and defense elsewhere.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: More lineup-managed than lineup-driving on defense right now.

summary.synopsis: Dynamic guard creator with real shotmaking gravity and a path to high-end on-ball offense.
strengths: Can create paint touches and tough shots against set defenses.
Jumper threat gives the handle real leverage.
Passing reads appear once he gets two on the ball.
weaknesses: Defensive value is currently more functional than additive.
Decision-making consistency will determine how much lead-guard equity he has.
swingFactors: Whether the passing sharpens into true primary-engine orchestration.
How much defensive resistance he can offer in playoff-sized lineups.

projection.bestOutcome: Primary guard scorer with enough passing to drive efficient offense.
projection.medianOutcome: High-level combo guard creator who can close games on offense.
projection.riskSummary: The offensive upside is clear, but the floor depends on shot selection and defensive survivability.

name: Cameron Boozer
teamFit: Fits pacey frontcourts that need size, rebounding, and quick decision-making.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Creates angles more with strength and touch than shake.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Rarely looks rushed for a young high-usage forward.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Can keep offense moving and punish help reads.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Not a finished spacer yet, but enough touch to project improvement.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Rebounding, screening, and finishing travel cleanly.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Plays with unusual feel for pace, timing, and counters.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Can fit with multiple frontcourt partners because the feel is real.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Should survive across frontcourt matchups if the mobility holds.

summary.synopsis: Polished young forward with mature feel, interior productivity, and real lineup versatility.
strengths: Advanced processing and poise for a freshman big forward.
Finishes possessions with rebounding and functional strength.
Can play within structure without needing offense built around him.
weaknesses: Perimeter shooting needs to become more respected to maximize spacing value.
Not a pure burst athlete, so advantage creation has to stay skill-based.
swingFactors: Whether the jumper becomes a true forcing function for defenses.
How much frontcourt defensive range he can carry at NBA speed.

projection.bestOutcome: High-level two-way frontcourt hub who closes games in multiple lineup shapes.
projection.medianOutcome: Winning starting four with offensive feel and reliable rebounding.
projection.riskSummary: The floor is strong; the upside depends on how much spacing and switch value arrive.

name: Caleb Wilson
teamFit: Best next to a mobile frontcourt partner or within aggressive help-heavy defenses.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Gets to productive spots more with length and activity than pure handle.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Generally keeps the game simple and on time.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Useful connective passer, not yet a creator hub.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Range remains more projective than proven.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Cuts, finishes, and activity play cleanly.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Rotations and reactions tend to be ahead of average frontcourt prospects.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Defensive range gives him multiple frontcourt fits.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: The main selling point is frontcourt coverage range and disruption.

summary.synopsis: Mobile, toolsy frontcourt defender whose value starts with coverage flexibility and activity.
strengths: Real defensive playmaking and movement for his size.
Off-ball offense fits modern frontcourt roles.
Can play with another big without clogging defensive scheme options.
weaknesses: Half-court offensive creation is still limited.
The jumper needs to become trustworthy to unlock bigger offensive ceiling.
swingFactors: Whether the shooting becomes playable enough to hold spacing.
How much physical growth he adds without losing movement.

projection.bestOutcome: Impact two-way frontcourt defender with enough offensive skill to stay on the floor deep in games.
projection.medianOutcome: Starting or high-minute rotation big built around defense and connective offense.
projection.riskSummary: The defensive base is strong, but the offensive ceiling depends heavily on the jumper stabilizing.

name: Kingston Flemings
teamFit: Best with shooting around him and a secondary wing defender handling the toughest perimeter assignment.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Gets two feet in the paint and shifts pace effectively.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Organized style keeps possessions on schedule.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Creates clean passes without overdribbling the advantage away.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Enough pull-up utility to keep defenders honest.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Primary value still starts with on-ball orchestration.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Reads develop quickly and usually stay connected to the right pass.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Needs the jumper and defense to hold up for easy lineup fit.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Defensive floor is more about effort than true coverage flexibility.

summary.synopsis: Steady lead-guard prospect built around pace control, passing organization, and pragmatic shot creation.
strengths: Plays under control and keeps the game connected.
Gets the offense into actions quickly.
Shows enough scoring threat to make the passing matter.
weaknesses: Does not have overwhelming physical tools on defense.
Must prove the jumper can carry bigger NBA usage pressure.
swingFactors: Whether the shooting becomes a true guard advantage creator.
How much defensive resistance he can generate at point of attack.

projection.bestOutcome: Winning starting point guard who keeps offense organized and efficient.
projection.medianOutcome: Rotation lead guard or steady co-creator in skill-first lineups.
projection.riskSummary: The feel is bankable, but the role size depends on how much scoring and point-of-attack defense arrive.

name: Darius Acuff
teamFit: Best with shooting around him and a secondary wing defender handling the toughest perimeter assignment.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Small-space creation and live-dribble pressure are real strengths.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Can tilt toward scoring mode before the simplest pass.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Sees kick-outs and pocket passes once he collapses the lane.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Shotmaking matters, though consistency remains important.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Profile is still much more on-ball than connective.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Can make high-difficulty reads, but the possession control is still variable.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Lineup fit tightens if the ball-dominant usage does not translate.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Size limits the defensive pathways.

summary.synopsis: Electric downhill guard with real paint pressure and live-dribble passing upside.
strengths: Can crack defenses off the bounce.
Passing flashes are strong when the first advantage is created.
Brings the kind of guard burst that changes defensive priorities.
weaknesses: Undersized guards need exceptional decision quality, and he is not there yet.
Defensive projection is role-limiting unless he materially overperforms physically.
swingFactors: Whether the jumper and decision-making make him efficient enough to lead offense.
How well he survives being targeted on the other end.

projection.bestOutcome: Explosive lead guard creator who drives offense with paint touches and pull-up threat.
projection.medianOutcome: Microwave scoring guard with real passing utility.
projection.riskSummary: The talent pop is obvious, but small-guard margin for error makes the efficiency questions central.

name: Mikel Brown
teamFit: Best with shooting around him and a secondary wing defender handling the toughest perimeter assignment.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Gets into offense smoothly and creates enough separation to run actions.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Usually sees the next pass and keeps pace under control.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Can create shots for others without overextending.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Jumper is a meaningful part of the offensive profile.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Can play off another creator if the jumper keeps translating.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Generally plays one beat ahead rather than one beat late.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Size helps the offensive fit more than the defensive one.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: More neutral than clearly additive defensively.

summary.synopsis: Skilled guard prospect with a balanced scoring-passing profile and clean offensive projection.
strengths: Comfortable running offense without hijacking possessions.
Jumper gives him both on-ball and off-ball utility.
Size for a lead guard helps the offensive translation.
weaknesses: Does not currently profile as a true physical pressure defender.
Needs enough rim pressure to keep the guard archetype from flattening.
swingFactors: Whether the first-step burst is enough to create consistent paint leverage.
How much defensive value he can add beyond just being playable.

projection.bestOutcome: Efficient lead guard who can score, organize, and play within a good offense.
projection.medianOutcome: Starting-caliber combo initiator with shooting and connective passing.
projection.riskSummary: The feel is there, but his ceiling depends on getting enough downhill pressure against NBA athletes.

name: Keaton Wagler
teamFit: Best with shooting around him and a secondary wing defender handling the toughest perimeter assignment.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: More pace-and-skill creator than power driver.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: One of the cleaner decision-makers in this guard cluster.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Keeps actions connected and can hit the next read.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: The jumper is already a real pressure point.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Can scale as a movement shooter and secondary attacker.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Usually gets to the right decision quickly.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Big guard shooting and decision-making fit nearly any lineup.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Size helps, but the defensive outcome still needs proving.

summary.synopsis: Big guard shooter with real feel and one of the cleaner scalability cases near the top of the board.
strengths: Jumper pressure changes how defenses can cover him.
Decision-making supports both on-ball and off-ball roles.
Size gives him easier lineup fit than smaller guards.
weaknesses: Needs enough burst to keep defenders from flattening the handle.
Defensive value is still more theoretical than banked.
swingFactors: Whether the handle generates enough paint pressure to unlock primary reps.
How much two-way impact comes with added strength.

projection.bestOutcome: Big lead guard or elite secondary creator whose shooting makes him easy to build around.
projection.medianOutcome: High-value combo guard who closes games because the decision-making and shot fit everywhere.
projection.riskSummary: The floor is strong because of size and shooting; the bigger upside depends on how much self-creation arrives.

name: Brayden Burries
teamFit: Best with shooting around him and a secondary wing defender handling the toughest perimeter assignment.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Gets to pull-ups and paint touches with strong pace changes.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Still balancing scorer instincts with control.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Can create the next pass but is wired to score first.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Shotmaking is a major reason he sits this high on the board.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Can function off another handler because the jumper carries.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: The offense still tilts toward difficult self-created solutions.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Shotmaking gives him an offensive lane even if he is not full-time lead.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Physical competitiveness matters because the defensive tools are not overwhelming.

summary.synopsis: Scoring-minded combo guard with enough shotmaking to carry real offensive upside.
strengths: Can manufacture offense late in possessions.
Jumper pressure changes the coverage.
Has enough strength to survive physical guard play.
weaknesses: Creator balance is still tilted more toward difficult scoring than orchestration.
Defensive ceiling looks more neutral than driving.
swingFactors: Whether the passing and possession management become cleaner.
How much point-of-attack competitiveness translates at the next level.

projection.bestOutcome: High-end scoring combo guard who can swing playoff possessions with shotmaking.
projection.medianOutcome: Offensive-minded secondary guard with starter-level scoring punch.
projection.riskSummary: The scoring bet is real, but the role size depends on whether he can drive efficient offense instead of just difficult offense.

name: Yaxel Lendeborg
teamFit: Fits pacey frontcourts that need size, rebounding, and quick decision-making.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Creates more with feel, power, and second-effort than pure burst.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Processes well for a frontcourt player and rarely kills possessions.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Can pass on the move and keep short-roll offense alive.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Still more workable than feared, but not the offensive driver.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Rebounding, cutting, screening, and activity all translate.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Feel is one of the strongest parts of the profile.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Can fit next to multiple stars because he does the connective work.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Brings real frontcourt resistance and utility.

summary.synopsis: Mature, productive frontcourt connector whose feel and two-way utility give him one of the safer role pathways on the board.
strengths: Processes the game quickly on both ends.
Adds rebounding and physicality without needing touches.
Can keep offense moving as a connective big.
weaknesses: Older prospect, so upside questions will follow him.
Jumper still needs to prove it can fully stretch NBA spacing.
swingFactors: Whether the shot becomes good enough to stay out of the way in every lineup.
How much his mature feel offsets the lower runway than freshman bets.

projection.bestOutcome: Winning starting four/five connector who anchors rotation lineups with feel, defense, and rebounding.
projection.medianOutcome: High-minute rotation frontcourt piece who improves lineup function on both ends.
projection.riskSummary: The floor is strong because the role utility is clear; the ceiling depends on whether the spacing holds.

name: Labaron Philon
teamFit: Best with shooting around him and a secondary wing defender handling the toughest perimeter assignment.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Wins paint touches with pacing changes, angles, and patient downhill craft.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Keeps possessions organized and usually sees the clean early read.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Can create advantage passes off live dribble without overextending the action.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Shot is credible enough to keep defenses attached, but not yet a true warper.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Value is still strongest when he is steering the offense.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Processes quickly and rarely looks sped up by the possession.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Feel gives him lineup portability, but the jumper still determines how easy the fit is.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Competitive guard defender, though not a size-driven stopper.

summary.synopsis: Steady secondary lead guard prospect whose value starts with pace control, passing organization, and low-chaos possession management.
strengths: Runs offense with maturity and keeps teammates involved.
Passing reads arrive early enough to keep actions flowing.
Gets to workable paint touches without needing elite burst.
weaknesses: Needs the jumper to become more forceful to unlock higher-usage pathways.
Defensive value is functional rather than lineup-shifting.
swingFactors: Whether the pull-up threat becomes strong enough to bend coverages.
How much point-of-attack resistance he can offer against bigger guards.

projection.bestOutcome: Winning lead guard who organizes offense, limits mistakes, and closes games with composed advantage creation.
projection.medianOutcome: Rotation lead or co-creator who stabilizes second units and fits skill-first lineups.
projection.riskSummary: The floor is strong because the feel is real; the ceiling depends on the shot carrying bigger offensive leverage.

name: Chris Cenac
teamFit: Best next to a mobile frontcourt partner or within aggressive help-heavy defenses.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Offense still comes more from tools, length, and finishing than self-created polish.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Keeps things simple and generally plays within the possession.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Mostly a play-finisher and connector rather than a true passing hub.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Spacing projection remains more long-term than current.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Rim pressure, offensive glass, and activity give him immediate utility.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Defensive reads and recovery reactions are encouraging for his age.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Can slot into lineups that need size, mobility, and low-maintenance offense.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Coverage range and playmaking are the core selling points.

summary.synopsis: Toolsy frontcourt bet whose value starts with defensive range, rim deterrence, and modern off-ball big utility.
strengths: Brings real defensive event creation for a young big.
Covers ground well enough to fit more than one scheme.
Finishing and activity play already look translatable.
weaknesses: Half-court offensive creation is still limited.
Shooting value is mostly projection today.
swingFactors: Whether the jumper becomes playable enough to hold spacing.
How much offensive processing he adds beyond finishing and connective reads.

projection.bestOutcome: Impact two-way big who can anchor aggressive defensive coverages while staying playable offensively.
projection.medianOutcome: Starting or high-minute rotation big built on defense, activity, and vertical utility.
projection.riskSummary: The defensive base is compelling, but the offensive ceiling still depends on skill growth rather than current creation.

name: Amari Allen
teamFit: Fits switch-heavy groups that need a wing connector next to on-ball talent.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Can attack tilted defenses, but not yet a primary on-ball problem solver.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Usually stays within the read and keeps the game moving.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Useful secondary passer without major creation burden.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Projects as a respectable spacing wing if the jumper settles.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Cuts, fills lanes, and complements star usage well.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Reacts quickly enough to play a connective role on both ends.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Wing size plus low-maintenance offense should fit around higher-end talent.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Has the tools to guard across wing assignments and survive switches.

summary.synopsis: Projectable two-way wing whose strongest pathway is as a scalable connector rather than an offense-driver.
strengths: Wing size and movement give him real lineup utility.
Plays comfortably off the ball and does not need many touches.
Defensive tools should translate into multi-assignment value.
weaknesses: On-ball creation ceiling is still limited.
Needs the jumper to stabilize to maximize the 3-and-D-plus outcome.
swingFactors: Whether the shooting becomes clearly respected by NBA defenses.
How much self-creation arrives to raise the offensive ceiling.

projection.bestOutcome: High-end two-way wing connector who closes games because the fit is easy on both ends.
projection.medianOutcome: Rotation wing with scalable defense and enough shooting to stay in lineups.
projection.riskSummary: The role floor is attractive, but the long-term value depends on whether the jumper becomes a true lineup-strength enhancer.
